label: Iran Killed
source: Hengaw / HRANA / Iran Health Ministry / Iran International / Al Jazeera
contestNote: Hengaw 7th report (Mar 25): 6,530 total killed incl. 640 confirmed civilians. HRANA (Day 34): 3,461+ killed incl. 1,551 civilians, 244 children; 8 civilians killed April 1 alone. Iran International (Apr 1): 4,700+ IRGC/security forces killed. Iran Red Crescent: 24,800+ injured. WHO: 18 hospitals struck; 190+ health/medical centers hit. 3.2M Iranians displaced (UNHCR). Apr 6 (Day 38): Iran's top university bombed — 34 killed in deadliest single-site civilian infrastructure attack of the conflict. Internet blackout (~1% connectivity / 840+ hrs cumulative) severely limits verification.

label: US Killed
contestNote: Pentagon official figure: 13 KIA, 300+ wounded. The Intercept (Apr 1-2) alleges Pentagon cover-up with ~750 total US killed/wounded — Pentagon denies; Congressional IG investigation requested. 13 KIA: 6 Army Reserve (Kuwait, Feb 28); Sgt. Pennington (Mar 8); 6 KC-135 crew (Iraq crash, Mar 13). Apr 6: Confirmed — US military jets shot down by Iranian enemy fire, first US aircraft losses to hostile fire in 20+ years. At least 2 aircraft lost; both pilots recovered alive. One aircraft deliberately destroyed to prevent Iranian capture. Iran air defenses remain lethal after 38 days of SEAD operations.

label: Israel Killed
source: IDF Spokesperson / Israeli health authorities / Times of Israel
deltaNote: 19+ killed in Israel total. 4,800+ wounded. Mar 28 (Day 29): Iranian missile struck Eshtaol (Jerusalem area) — IDF interception failure; several lightly injured. Houthi missile toward Beersheba triggered sirens. Mar 27: 1 IDF soldier killed by Lebanese rockets; IDF Chief Zamir warns of 15,000-troop shortfall. Iran's pre-stockpiled missiles still penetrating Israeli defenses despite production destroyed.

label: Gulf Region Killed
deltaNote: 22+ total across Gulf states. Mar 24: Iran struck fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport, sparking fire; Saudi Arabia and UAE reportedly 'losing patience' and may join strikes on Iran (Bloomberg). UAE Shah Gas Field attacked Mar 18 (suspended operations). Qatar LNG remains halted Day 27. Saudi Arabia intercepted 37+ drones. 16+ vessels struck in Persian Gulf.

label: Lebanon Killed
source: Lebanese health authorities / Lebanon Information Ministry / Al Jazeera
deltaNote: 1,156+ killed including 130+ children; 1M+ displaced. Apr 5: Israeli strikes across multiple Lebanese locations kill at least 14 more. Mar 28: IDF struck Beirut southern suburbs (Dahiyeh) and Deir al-Zahrani; Al Manar/Al Mayadeen journalists killed; Lebanese President Aoun condemned strikes. IDF has declared 30km security zone to Litani River.

label: Brent Crude
source: Reuters / Al Jazeera / OilPrice.com / CNBC / Goldman Sachs / Bloomberg
deltaNote: Brent ~$110/bbl Day 34 (up from ~$107 Day 32) amid April 6 deadline tension. IEA (Apr 1): April crunch will be 'much worse than March.' Macquarie: 40% probability Brent hits $200/bbl. April oil cliff approaching mid-April as SPR buffer exhausted. Over March as a whole: Brent up 60%+ — biggest monthly gain on record since 1980s. US gas avg $4.02/gal. Apr 6 Hormuz deadline firm.

label: Flights Cancelled
deltaNote: ~500+ tankers stranded in Persian Gulf. Qatar LNG offline (Day 2) — global LNG and fertilizer supply chains disrupted. Hormuz selective passage: Iran allowing China/India/Pakistan-flagged vessels under coordination. 20M bbl/day backed up; 5M diverted to alternate routes. UN FAO emergency alert on food price spike. European natural gas TTF +22% cumulative since war onset. Slovenia: first EU fuel rationing. Spain: €5B emergency energy aid.

label: Countries Affected
source: Al Jazeera / Reuters / France 24 / CNN / NewsNation
deltaNote: Iran, Israel, Lebanon, UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar (tanker struck off Doha Apr 1), Saudi Arabia, Oman, Iraq, Jordan, Yemen (Houthis), Azerbaijan, Turkey, Cyprus, France, Switzerland, Pakistan (intermediary), Slovenia, Thailand, Malaysia. Day 33-34: Trump ceasefire claim vs Iran's 5-condition war-end proposal; Kharazi struck (Pakistan diplomatic channel disrupted); new missile wave at Israel; IEA 'April cliff' warning; UK 35-nation Hormuz conference; Apr 6 deadline 4 days away.
